summary:
The “needle” causing the failure was a shutdown-ordering deadlock in Scheduler.Run:

1. It waited for the worker goroutine to finish (`<-done`)
2. Only afterwards did it close the queue (`s.queue.Close()`)

If the worker was blocked inside `Pop()`, it could never notice `ctx.Done()` until the queue closed, so both goroutines could wait forever.

Fix applied:

• Reverse the order – on cancellation, close the queue first to unblock any `Pop()` calls, then wait for the worker goroutine.

```go
<-ctx.Done()

// Close queue first to release any Pop() waiters.
s.queue.Close()

// Now it’s safe to wait for the worker to exit.
<-done
```

Running `test.sh` now prints:

OK: clean shutdown  
PASS
